Revision History Changed By Date Version Notes: Christopher Veau 11/29/2022 0.0 Original document. EX 67 – Item Selection List Currently Active p icking occurs in either Dematic or via Pick Sheet. With the implementation of EX33 , Dematic is phased out and that side of Active picking is brought into the WMOS fold . With this change the use of Pick To Tote and Pack From Tote transactions are leveraged in order to carry out the Chase wave picking for inventory that was not initially picked with Pack Pick Cart. Similarly to P ack Pick Cart , when users are executing the Pick To Tote transaction, they may run into unscannable item barcodes . WMOS is customized to give the users an option where they are able to see the list of items that are yet to be picked for the task and be able select one of them. On the Pack From Tote transaction WMOS is customized to give the users an option where they are able to see the list of items that are in the Tote and be able to select one of them. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y Custom screens for Pick To Tote and Pack From Tote Reporting N Other N RF Pick To Tote and RF Pack From Tote are customized with the addition of a Ctrl-L option on their respective item prompt screens. The Ctrl-L option gives the users a list of items that they are able to select from. For the Pick To Tote transaction this list of items consists of the items for task details that have yet to be completed. For the Pack From Tote transaction the list of items consists of the items which are associated with the oLPN scanned and that have not been packed out yet. Assumptions This extension modifies the Pick To Tote and P a ck From Tote transaction s only . Cigarette Picking does not occur through Pack Pick Cart and therefore also does not occur through Pick To Tote and P a ck From Tote. Chase for Active Items picked with Pack Pick Cart occurs at the oLPN level . This means oLPNs waiting on chased items are set aside at a designated area/location to receive their chase picks and then are packed and move d to loading. RF Pickers have hip printers to print oLPN shipping label during picking. Base Task Group Validations occur when P ick to Tote or Pack From Tote are entered through tasking. Alternate pick location capabilities are not used by McLane in RF Pick to Tote flow s and are not in scope of this extension. For EX 67 RF Pick to Tote Transaction Parameters the following configuration is needed : 1-1 Item Verification Mode = Verify pick by scanning item and qty For EX67 RF P ack From Tote Transaction Parameters the following configuration is needed : 1-1 Pack From Tote Mode = Pack oLPN from Tote (RF & MHE) 3-3 Item Verification Mode = Scan item and quant ity OR Scan item During EX33 Pack Pick Cart u sers apply printed Shipping Labels to totes Reprinting o L PN Shipping Labels occurs through standard base methods. No additional custom printing is in scope for this extension. McLane pick locations are not dynamic. Dependencies Code Dependencies MCLN EX33 RF Picking for Active Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No changes to base behavior for PIX generation as part of this extension. RF RF P ick To Tote Item Selection List Program Platform Available Text Based (RF Gun) Y UI (RF UI) Y Mobile (DM) N Base RF P ick To Tote transaction is enhanced with a Ctrl Key to allow users to view a list of items still needing to be picked with in the current task . The screen will be similar to the base Item selection screen , the user is then able to select a record from the list. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing occur s . Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 67 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX33 is turned off, and no custom EX 67 processing occur s . Last , the Custom RF transaction parameter on the RF P ick To Tote transaction m ust be enabled. If base transaction parameter for Item Verification Mode is set to ‘2’ – ‘ Verify Pick by scanning i tem and q uantity’ and custom transaction parameter 5 67 -5 67 is set to “Y”, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. If custom transaction parameter 5 67 -5 67 is set to “N”, or not set up at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base processing. Validations There are no validation s needed in order to use Ctrl-L in order to pull up the Item Selection List screen Display The below base item selection screen is leveraged to display the items that are found via the logic in section 6.1.5 Customized Screen Layout 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 12345678901234567890 Seq ItemDesc/ItemBU 1 XXXXXXXXXXXXXX 2 XXXXXXXXXXXXXX . . . S =>__ 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 12345678901234567890 Seq ItemDesc / ItemBU 1 XXXXXXXXXXXXXX 2 XXXXXXXXXXXXXX . . . S =>__ Line Number Literal Source Display Length (R/L justified) 1 ‘ Seq ItemDesc/ItemBU’ Text 20 L 2 - X ‘# ‘ Sequence number 2 L 2a - Xa Item description 18L X+1 ‘S=>’ Text 3L Ctrl Keys Ctrl Key MSG # Ctrl Key Text Action Ctrl-W CTRLKEY/1002 Previous Previous Ctrl-X CTRLKEY/1001 Exit Exit Navigation In order for the Item Selection List screen to be shown to the user, the user needs to use the custom CTRL-L option when on the RF Pick To Tote verify pick item and quantity prompt screen (shown below) Once the Item Selection List screen is shown to the user and the user has selected a record from th e l ist , they are brought to the V erify Pick item and quantity screen and should be prompted for the quantity. The Item Barcode field is to be populated with the item barcode of the item that was selected in the Item Selection List screen. Logic The list of items will com e from the current task and the details that have yet to be completed Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to the base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base labor hooks as part of this extension. RF Pack From Tote Item Selection List Program Platform Available Text Based (RF Gun) Y UI (RF UI) Y Mobile (DM) N Base RF P ack From Tote transaction is enhanced with a Ctrl Key to allow users to view a list of items still needing to be p a cked out within the tote . The screen will be similar to the base Item selection screen, the user is then able to select a record from the list. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing occurs. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX67 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX33 is turned off, and no custom EX67 processing occurs. Last, the Custom RF transaction parameter on the RF P ack From Tote transaction m ust be enabled. If base transaction parameter for Item Verification Mode is set to ‘2’ – ‘Verify Pick by scanning item and quantity’ and custom transaction parameter 567-567 is set to “Y”, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. If custom transaction parameter 567-567 is set to “N”, or not set up at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base processing. Validations There are no validations needed in order to use Ctrl-L in order to pull up the Item Selection List screen Display The below base item selection screen is leveraged to display the items that are found via the logic in section 6.1.5 Customized Screen Layout 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 12345678901234567890 Seq ItemDesc/ItemBU 1 XXXXXXXXXXXXXX 2 XXXXXXXXXXXXXX . . . S=>__ 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 12345678901234567890 Seq ItemDesc / ItemBU 1 XXXXXXXXXXXXXX 2 XXXXXXXXXXXXXX . . . S=>__ Line Number Literal Source Display Length (R/L justified) 1 ‘ Seq ItemDesc/ItemBU’ Text 20 L 2 - X ‘# ‘ Sequence number 2 L 2a - Xa Item description 18L X+1 ‘S=>’ Text 3L Ctrl Keys Ctrl Key MSG # Ctrl Key Text Action Ctrl-W CTRLKEY/1002 Previous Previous Ctrl-X CTRLKEY/1001 Exit Exit Navigation In order for the Item Selection List screen to be shown to the user, the user needs to use the custom CTRL-L option when on the RF P ack From Tote verify item screen (shown below) Once the Item Selection List screen is shown to the user and the user has selected a record from the list, they are brought to the Verify Pick item and quantity screen and should be prompted for the quantity. The Item Barcode field is to be populated with the item barcode of the item that was selected in the Item Selection List screen. Logic The list of items will come from the remaining oLPN details of the current oLPN that was scanned at the beginning of the transaction. Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to the base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base labor hooks as part of this extension. Process Change/Addition No additional process changes in scope for this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. Menu Parameters Parameter (Category > Parameter) Value Warehouse Master Parameter (Tab > Section > Parameter ) Value System Codes Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description Frm To Description Validation Type Configured value C 000 000 Global Extension Control Global Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – Global Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX 67 EX 67 Extension Control EX 67 Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX 67 Extension Control is turned ON Database Not required for this extension. Custom Configurations System Code Types Rec Type Code Type Code Desc RF Desc Max Len . Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing occur s . Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 67 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX33 is turned off, and no custom EX 67 processing occur s .

system code C000 code id EX 67 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX33 is turned off, and no custom EX 67 processing occur s .

Last , the Custom RF transaction parameter on the RF P ick To Tote transaction m ust be enabled. If base transaction parameter for Item Verification Mode is set to ‘2’ – ‘ Verify Pick by scanning i tem and q uantity’ and custom transaction parameter 5 67 -5 67 is set to “Y”, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. If custom transaction parameter 5 67 -5 67 is set to “N”, or not set up at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base processing. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing occurs. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX67 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX33 is turned off, and no custom EX67 processing occurs. Last, the Custom RF transaction parameter on the RF P ack From Tote transaction m ust be enabled. If base transaction parameter for Item Verification Mode is set to ‘2’ – ‘Verify Pick by scanning item and quantity’ and custom transaction parameter 567-567 is set to “Y”, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. If custom transaction parameter 567-567 is set to “N”, or not set up at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base processing.
